module_mood_contract: mechanical rescue, not triumph — only the groundedness sub-problem gets rescued; M3 strategic uncertainty and M4 security residual must stay alive

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood contract named.
Epistemic mood ≠ punitive mood.
Lead with the discipline, not the failure mode.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Title carries the failure word ('Hallucination benchmark') but the body leads with the discipline ('The move is empirical... You run the candidates, measure, and keep the one that caught what mattered'). Discipline-first in the body holds; title is paired by design across M5 surfaces.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategy-fidelity check.
Walk-away test.
50% magic rule — understandable magic.
Exercise quality ladder — design 9 for arc peaks (M5 rescue).
One exercise = one bounded activity (4 phases as one internal arc).
Teach the principle and the technique — wow-mood exception with same-module deconstruction.
Forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose.
Measure silent failure, not just friction.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Maintainer block names the silent-failure mode explicitly: 'The scoreboard looks clean and the student doesn't read it' — Phase 2 surprise-row gate forces the read.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Reflection beats prefer in-chat over file-write.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: All file writes are read downstream (briefing → extractor; claim-pool → detectors; detector files → scorer; scoreboard → judge-save; judge → M6).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body teaches the work; trainer manages the room outside text.
Cross-module artefact contracts must be explicit at both ends.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: judges/groundedness-judge.md named at production (Phase 3 prompt L227) and at hand-off ('Module 6 picks this file up' L215; 'Delineation with M6' in maintainer block).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Per-exercise leap test — three observable Monday-morning outcomes.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Maintainer block has Walk-away calibration, Mood check, Watch-fors, Push-back moves, Delineation with M6 — but no explicit numbered list of three observable Monday-morning outcomes the student exhibits on their own work. The 'Understandable magic bar' sentence is closest but is a verbal description, not three falsifiable named verbs.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Add a 'Leap test' row to the maintainer block: three falsifiable verbs the student does on their own output Monday — e.g. 'runs four candidate detectors against any over-reaching output of theirs', 'reads a precision/recall scoreboard and names which row drove the pick', 'saves a narrow judge file with a Known limit line for one shape of output'.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Cross-module artefact contracts as maintainer-block contract rows.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Delineation with M6 names the artefact (judges/groundedness-judge.md), the producing prompt (Phase 3), and the consuming module (M6). Could be tightened into a formal contract row but the substance is present.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Per-phase failure mode + escape hatch.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Watch-fors (5 named failures with coach lines) + Push-back moves (8 named beats with phase pointers) cover every phase's dominant failure with a recovery move.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Heavy fan-out prompts get a body-side recovery callout.
rules_evaluated.evidence: L100–112 spawns four parallel subagents (N>2 parallel = rule 48 signal). Body before the prompt says 'Four detectors, four different methods, run in parallel on the same claim pool. Each is a subagent with a specific lens.' and after says 'Four files in a minute or two.' No body callout names heavy-run-expected or the recovery move (interrupt → narrower steer → say 'continue'), and no paste-ready hard-cap example.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Add a one-line callout above the four-detector prompt: heavy fan-out is expected; if a detector over-reads or stalls, interrupt and steer narrower or say 'continue'. The run is bounded (30 claims × roster files), so the cap is structural, not a numeric ceiling — which itself is worth naming.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Meta-frame lectures are closers, not openers.
rules_evaluated.evidence: File is an exercise; lecture placement is decided in the module file (output-quality.md) which already places the meta-frame lecture (self-consistency-after-scoreboard.md) as a closer.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Minimal lecturing before contrast-mood exercises.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Exercise opener arms (the move is empirical / four detectors, one scoreboard) without front-loading vocabulary; precision/recall earn themselves at Phase 2 from the scoreboard rows.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood lands per phase + close (mood-floor 8+, <7 = REVISE; setup beats exempt).
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Phase scores 8/8/9/8/9; close 9. Surprise-row gate at Phase 2 close is the rescue beat and lands at 9.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood doesn't resolve early — M3 strategic uncertainty + M4 security residual stay alive.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Body L239 ('yours was 30 claims. A real production judge wants hundreds. ... That's next module.') keeps the scope narrow; Phase 3 prompt L229 forces a 'Known limit:' line on the judge file. Maintainer Mood check confirms M3/M4 residuals untouched.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Teaching moment lands across reasonable persona-skill variation.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Surprise-row gate (Phase 2 close) + 'name the row, name the number, name why' coach line in Watch-fors prevent skim-past for the fast-operator persona. Scoreboard adjudicates — student doesn't classify by hand — so technical confidence isn't a precondition.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Big Idea fidelity (output-quality.md M5 Big Idea).
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Module Big Idea: 'You don't pick a quality check because someone said so. You run several on your own output, score them against a 30-claim benchmark, and keep the winner.' Body L15 mirrors verbatim spirit ('The move is empirical. You don't pick a detection method because somebody said so. You run the candidates, measure, and keep the one that caught what mattered on your output.').

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Module-to-module arc — close hands off to next module's open.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Close L239 + maintainer Delineation with M6 frame the seam as hunger ('30 claims here; imagine hundreds running on every build'), not closure.
